commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(deploy): support subpath deployments via Next.js basePath (PUNT-292) (#412)

## Summary

- Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH` environment variable support for deploying
PUNT under a subpath (e.g., `example.com/punt/`)
- Create `src/lib/base-path.ts` with utilities (`basePath`,
`withBasePath()`, `assetUrl()`, `apiFetch()`) for basePath-aware URL
handling
- Update all client-side API calls (`fetch`, `EventSource`,
`window.location.href`, `new URL()`) across 64 files to use
basePath-aware helpers
- Update `AvatarImage` component to auto-prepend basePath to `/uploads/`
avatar URLs
- Update logo, branding, attachment, MCP config, and webhook URLs for
basePath support
- Update auth redirect URLs in `auth.config.ts` for basePath support

### What Next.js handles automatically (no changes needed):
- `<Link>` component hrefs
- `router.push()` / `router.replace()`
- `redirect()` from `next/navigation`
- `next/image` src
- Static file serving from `public/`

### What needed manual fixes:
- `fetch('/api/...')` calls (48 files) -> `apiFetch('/api/...')`
- `new EventSource('/api/...')` (3 hooks) -> `withBasePath('/api/...')`
- `window.location.href = '/...'` (8 locations) ->
`withBasePath('/...')`
- `new URL('/api/...', origin)` (3 locations) ->
`withBasePath('/api/...')`
- Avatar/logo/attachment image src attributes -> `withBasePath(url)`
- Auth middleware redirect URLs -> include basePath

## Test plan

- [ ] Deploy without `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH` set -- verify app works
identically to before (no regression)
- [ ] Deploy with `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH="/punt"` -- verify:
  - [x] Login/logout redirects work correctly
  - [x] API calls succeed (ticket CRUD, project operations)
  - [ ] SSE real-time updates connect properly
  - [ ] Avatar images load correctly
  - [ ] Logo/branding images load correctly
  - [ ] File attachments display and download correctly
  - [x] MCP config shows correct server URL with basePath
  - [ ] Webhook URL in project settings includes basePath

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add pending draft count badge to Communications sidebar item

Add a real-time badge on the Communications nav item showing the count
of actionable drafts (pending_review + draft status). Polls every 60s
with immediate updates on draft mutations (create, delete, send, batch).

- Backend: GET /drafts/counts endpoint with per-status breakdown
- Frontend: getDraftCounts API, usePendingDraftCount hook (60s poll)
- Sidebar: wired into existing mergedBadges system
- Draft priority scoring and original-email endpoint additions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix municipality city-level Region level attribute (#5)

* Fix municipality city-level Region to use correct level attribute

For municipalities/SARs (Beijing, Shanghai, etc.), lookup_adcode, reverse,
and reverse_batch now consistently populate the city field with a Region
whose level is set to 'city' (not 'province'), matching conventional Chinese
geocoding API behavior.

Previously, city was either None (inconsistent with other methods) or had
an incorrect level attribute. This fix ensures all three methods return
consistent, semantically correct results for municipalities.

* Add v2.0.0 migration guide for GB code to adcode transition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(coordinator): prefer PAT over GitHub App token when set

When GH_TOKEN_ALPHA/BETA/GAMMA env vars are set, use those PATs
instead of GitHub App tokens. This allows agents to operate under
separate GitHub accounts for full identity separation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove unused jitpack repo and IPFS dependency to fix Koyeb build timeout

jitpack.io repository was tried for every dependency (~20s timeout each),
causing build timeouts on Koyeb. The java-ipfs-http-client dependency was
never imported in code (IpfsService uses RestTemplate instead), so both
can be safely removed.

Also includes bean-definition-overriding fix for all profiles.

commit.message:
Fix Instagram portrait video audio by using width-based format filter

Reels are portrait (e.g. 720x1280) so height=1280 exceeds the quality
filter, causing yt-dlp to fall back to a video-only stream with no audio.
Switch to width-based filtering so "720p" correctly selects the 720x1280
DASH stream and merges it with the audio track.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add UX brainstorm features: reduce motion, clutter-free, print layout, mentions (#158)

## Summary
- **Reduce Motion Option**: Tri-state toggle (Auto/On/Off) respecting
`prefers-reduced-motion` OS preference, applies `data-reduce-motion`
attribute to suppress animations/transitions with 0.01ms durations
- **Clutter-Free Toggle**: `F` key hides all annotation markers for
distraction-free reading, adds "Export clean text" option for
annotation-free markdown export, persists setting to localStorage
- **Print-Optimized Layout**: Superscript `[cN]`/`[aN]` reference
markers in printed text linking to endnote annotations via ProseMirror
widget decorations, B&W-safe layer legend with bordered dots
- **@Mentions in Comments**: `@name` autocomplete in comment editor
using TipTap Mention extension, combines Yjs awareness users + document
members API, styled mention spans in rendered comments

## Test plan
- [x] Build passes (`bun run build`) — zero errors
- [x] All 2008 unit tests pass (`bun run test:run`)
- [x] Lint clean (`bun run lint`) — zero errors
- [x] E2E tests pass (2 pre-existing failures in arrow-drawing unrelated
to this PR)
- [x] Reduce Motion: `data-reduce-motion` attribute set correctly for
auto/on/off states
- [x] Clutter-Free: annotations hidden/shown via F key, displayLayers
computed correctly
- [x] Print Layout: `buildPrintRefMap` numbering logic tested (7 tests),
decorations applied
- [x] Mentions: `useMentionableUsers` hook tested,
`MentionSuggestionList` rendering tested

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

#27: Support lowercase characters and shift-modified output (#30)

* Support lowercase characters and shift-modified output

Change KeyCode letter values from uppercase ASCII (0x41-0x5A) to
lowercase (0x61-0x7A) as the default. Add resolveShift() to map keys
to their shifted variants (uppercase letters, shifted symbols like
! @ # etc). The driver applies shift/caps lock logic before dispatching:
letters use shift XOR caps_lock, symbols use shift alone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add explicit uppercase letter KeyCode entries (A-Z)

Rename the lowercase letter entries from A-Z to a-z (matching ASCII
semantics) and add uppercase A-Z at 0x41-0x5A. The scancode table
and resolveShift now use the named enum values throughout, eliminating
raw static_casts for letter case conversion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify resolveShift letter case to a range check with offset

Replace the 26-case switch for a-z with a range check and subtract
0x20, matching the ASCII uppercase/lowercase relationship directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Add self-contained binary with embedded UI, flexible CSV parser, and multi-client support

- Embed both scorecard and cockpit React UIs in the binary (view.go, embed/)
- After import, auto-run cockpit and serve both UIs locally with two browser tabs
- Flexible CSV header parsing: column-name lookup instead of exact string match,
  survives Zerodha format changes (extra columns, reordering, spacing)
- EQ parser rejects F&O files (expiry_date column detection)
- Support 2-3 letter client ID prefixes (e.g. DUA527) in regex and --client flag
- Scorecard UI supports ?client= parameter for multi-client viewing
- Add deploy-friend.sh for optional GitHub Pages deployment

commit.message:
Fix: migrate design tokens from :root to @theme for Tailwind 4 compatibility

Tailwind 4 cannot resolve arbitrary CSS variable values (text-[var(--xxx)])
at build time — no CSS was generated, causing all styling to fall back to
browser defaults. Moved all tokens to @theme directive and replaced broken
patterns with native Tailwind utilities across all 14 files.

commit.message:
Add gRPC health checks to router and server

Use tonic-health to serve grpc.health.v1 on both binaries. The server
registers cache.CacheService as SERVING after loading the on-disk index
and flips to NOT_SERVING on SIGTERM before tonic drains in-flight
requests. The router registers cache.RouterService as SERVING on start
and NOT_SERVING on shutdown.

Helm readiness probes switch from tcpSocket to native grpc probes with
explicit service names. The router deployment gains a readiness probe
(previously had none).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add manual rotation feature to Big Dipper Clock (v1.1.0)

New interactive feature allowing users to manually rotate the star field
and automatically calculate the matching time. Users can now match the
simulation to their actual observation of the night sky.

Features added:
1. Mouse drag rotation
   - Click and drag on canvas to rotate stars
   - Smooth real-time rotation
   - Cursor feedback (grab/grabbing)
   - Auto-pauses animation when dragging

2. Rotation control buttons
   - ↻ CW: Rotate clockwise (backward in time)
   - ↺ CCW: Rotate counterclockwise (forward in time)
   - 15° per click = ~1 hour
   - Auto-pauses animation when clicked

3. Time offset display
   - Shows rotation offset (e.g., "+2h 30m")
   - Updates in real-time
   - Clearly indicates time deviation

4. Automatic time calculation
   - Date/time inputs update automatically during rotation
   - Accurate sidereal day conversion (23h 56m = 360°)
   - Handles multi-day rotations
   - Rotation direction follows natural sky motion

5. Smart mode management
   - Manual rotation pauses animation
   - Manual time change resets rotation
   - Step buttons reset rotation
   - Reset button clears all rotation

Technical implementation:
- Added rotation state tracking (offset, baseTime, drag state)
- Mouse event handlers for canvas dragging
- Rotation-to-time conversion (360° = 86,164 seconds)
- Auto-pause animation on rotation start
- Time offset calculation and display
- Canvas cursor styling

Use case:
Users can observe the real night sky, rotate the simulation to match
what they see, and read the current time from the updated fields.

Files modified:
- index.html: Added manual rotation controls UI
- js/main.js: Added rotation logic and event handlers
- MANUAL_ROTATION_FEATURE.md: Complete feature documentation

commit.message:
fix(gateway): terminate WebSocket immediately on abort to prevent shutdown timeout

Replace ws.close() with ws.terminate() in the abort handler so the TCP socket
is destroyed instantly without waiting for a close-frame handshake. Avoids
lingering event-loop handles that caused the gateway to log "shutdown timed out;
exiting without full cleanup" when the Satori server had already disconnected
(1006 abnormal closure).

commit.message:
Replace $unwind aggregation pipelines with EventStaff collection queries for scale

Rewrites 7 $unwind pipelines in ai.ts and staff.ts to query EventStaff collection
directly, eliminating O(n*m) intermediate document explosion at scale. Also batches
timesheet reminders and fixes rate limiter validation for express-rate-limit v8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite scoring: "can I make money?" instead of "can I be #1?"

Fundamental shift in analysis philosophy from measuring dominance
potential to measuring realistic profitability for new entrants:

Scoring changes:
- Competition: replace "Entrenched Leaders" (penalized big brands)
  with "Small Seller Viability" (counts <500-review products selling)
- Quality Gap: raise floor from 10→40 when quality is uniformly high
  (high quality bar ≠ no opportunity, just the standard to match)
- New "Entrant Viability" score (10% of final): monthly profit for
  new seller, % small sellers surviving, best entry ease
- Reweight: 30% Demand + 25% Competition + 25% Price + 10% Quality
  + 10% Entrant Viability (was 30/30/20/20)

Revenue & entry improvements:
- 3 revenue tiers: Top (p90 leaders), Mid (p50 established),
  Entry (p25 new seller) — replaces single misleading number
- Entry ease uses median reviews (not mean, which leaders inflated)
  with lower thresholds (150/500 vs 200/800)
- Price ranges now show small_sellers count (<300 reviews)

Frontend:
- 5 score gauges (added Viabilidad Entrante)
- 3 revenue cards with labels (Entrada/Medio/Top)
- 5th breakdown section for entrant viability
- Price opportunity table shows small sellers column

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

V10.9: 실적캘린더 2주+요일 표시 + 어닝콜 트랜스크립트 검색 기능

- [실적캘린더] 3개월 → 2주 표시 (이번주+다음주)
- [실적캘린더] 날짜 형식 변경: '월 03/09' → '03/09 (월)'
- [어닝콜] 트랜스크립트 전문(full_text) 세션 저장
- [어닝콜] 검색 기능: 키워드 입력 → AI가 정확한 원문 quotation + 한국어 맥락 설명 반환
- [어닝콜] 검색 결과 expander로 표시, 여러 검색 누적 가능
